test_cases.name: happy_path_standard_software_project
test_cases.scenario: Complete risk register generation for a standard software development project with clear scope, moderate risk appetite, and historical data available.

input.scope_document:
Project: E-commerce Platform Redesign
Objectives: Modernize legacy platform, improve user experience, reduce load times by 50%
Deliverables: New frontend (React), API layer (Node.js), database migration, user documentation
Constraints: Timeline 6 months (Jan-Jun 2025), Budget $500K, 8-person team, must maintain backward compatibility
Stakeholders: Product Manager, CTO, 3 business units, 2 external vendors
Success Criteria: 95% uptime, <2s page load, zero data loss, user adoption >80%

similar_projects.name: Mobile App Redesign 2023
similar_projects.lessons_learned: API integration delays common; underestimated testing effort; vendor communication critical

similar_projects.name: Database Migration 2022
similar_projects.lessons_learned: Data validation is critical; rollback planning essential; stakeholder alignment prevents scope creep

input.stakeholder_input: Concern: vendor reliability for payment gateway integration. Concern: team has limited React experience.

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill generates complete risk register with 20-40 identified risks, at least 5 high-priority risks, all phases executed, mitigation strategies for high-priority risks, EWIs with measurable thresholds, and markdown summary.
phase_coverage: Phase 1: Extract and Analyze Project Scope
Phase 2: Identify Risk Categories and Brainstorm Risks
Phase 3: Assess Probability and Impact
Phase 4: Calculate Priority and Rank Risks
Phase 5: Develop Mitigation Strategies
Phase 6: Define Early Warning Indicators
Phase 7: Compile and Validate Risk Register

test_cases.name: happy_path_infrastructure_project
test_cases.scenario: Risk register for infrastructure/cloud migration project with aggressive timeline and conservative risk appetite.

input.scope_document:
Project: Cloud Migration (On-Prem to AWS)
Objectives: Move 50 applications to AWS, reduce infrastructure costs by 40%, improve disaster recovery
Deliverables: Cloud architecture design, migration runbooks, security compliance validation, training materials
Constraints: Timeline 4 months (Feb-May 2025), Budget $300K, 5-person team, zero downtime required, SOC2 compliance mandatory
Stakeholders: CIO, Security Officer, Finance, 4 application owners
Success Criteria: All apps migrated, <1 hour RTO, zero data loss, SOC2 certified

similar_projects.name: AWS Migration Phase 1
similar_projects.lessons_learned: Network latency issues; compliance validation took longer than expected; vendor support critical

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill generates risk register with infrastructure-specific risks (network, security, compliance, vendor), numeric probability/impact ratings (1-5 scale), conservative high-priority threshold (score ≥4), detailed mitigation strategies with named owners and timelines.
phase_coverage: Phase 1: Extract and Analyze Project Scope
Phase 2: Identify Risk Categories and Brainstorm Risks
Phase 3: Assess Probability and Impact
Phase 4: Calculate Priority and Rank Risks
Phase 5: Develop Mitigation Strategies
Phase 6: Define Early Warning Indicators
Phase 7: Compile and Validate Risk Register

test_cases.name: edge_case_minimal_scope_document
test_cases.scenario: Risk register generation with minimal scope document (missing some elements) to test graceful handling of incomplete input.

input.scope_document:
Project: Internal Tool Development
Objectives: Build reporting dashboard
Deliverables: Dashboard UI, backend API
Timeline: 3 months

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill identifies missing scope elements (budget, stakeholders, constraints, success criteria), documents clarification questions, proceeds with available scope, flags risks related to scope ambiguity (e.g., 'Ambiguous requirements may cause rework'), marks register as 'draft pending scope clarification'.
phase_coverage: Phase 1: Extract and Analyze Project Scope
Phase 2: Identify Risk Categories and Brainstorm Risks
Phase 3: Assess Probability and Impact
Phase 4: Calculate Priority and Rank Risks
Phase 7: Compile and Validate Risk Register

test_cases.name: edge_case_high_risk_count_consolidation
test_cases.scenario: Risk brainstorming yields >100 risks; skill must consolidate overlapping risks and deprioritize low-impact items to target 20-50 risks.

input.scope_document:
Project: Enterprise ERP Implementation
Objectives: Replace 5 legacy systems with single ERP platform
Deliverables: System configuration, data migration, user training, custom integrations
Constraints: Timeline 12 months, Budget $2M, 20-person team, 50+ stakeholders, 100+ integrations
Stakeholders: CFO, COO, 5 department heads, 50+ end users
Success Criteria: 95% uptime, zero data loss, user adoption >90%, cost <$2M

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill identifies >100 risks during brainstorming, consolidates duplicates and overlaps (e.g., 'team skill gap' and 'lack of expertise' merged), deprioritizes low-impact risks to watch list, produces final register with 20-50 risks, documents watch list separately, focuses detailed mitigation on top 20-30 risks.
phase_coverage: Phase 2: Identify Risk Categories and Brainstorm Risks
Phase 3: Assess Probability and Impact
Phase 4: Calculate Priority and Rank Risks
Phase 5: Develop Mitigation Strategies
Phase 7: Compile and Validate Risk Register

test_cases.name: error_handling_incomplete_scope_with_recovery
test_cases.scenario: Scope document is missing critical elements (budget, timeline); skill documents clarification questions and proceeds with assumptions.

input.scope_document:
Project: Research Initiative
Objectives: Investigate machine learning applications for fraud detection
Deliverables: Research report, proof-of-concept model
Stakeholders: Research team, fraud prevention team

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill detects missing budget and timeline, documents specific clarification questions ('What is the project budget?' 'What is the target completion date?'), proceeds with available scope, flags scope-related risks ('Ambiguous timeline may cause schedule overrun'), marks register as 'draft pending scope clarification', recommends follow-up before execution.
phase_coverage: Phase 1: Extract and Analyze Project Scope
Phase 2: Identify Risk Categories and Brainstorm Risks
Phase 3: Assess Probability and Impact
Phase 7: Compile and Validate Risk Register

test_cases.name: quality_gate_probability_basis_documentation
test_cases.scenario: Risk assessment includes both historical data and expert judgment; probability ratings are justified with documented basis.

input.scope_document:
Project: Mobile App Launch
Objectives: Launch iOS and Android app for retail customers
Deliverables: iOS app, Android app, backend API, marketing materials
Constraints: Timeline 6 months, Budget $400K, 6-person team, must support 1M users at launch
Stakeholders: Product Manager, Marketing, iOS/Android leads, CTO
Success Criteria: 4.5+ star rating, <2s load time, zero critical bugs at launch

similar_projects.name: Previous App Launch 2023
similar_projects.lessons_learned: Performance testing critical; app store review delays; user adoption slower than expected

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill documents probability basis for each risk: 'Historical (X% of similar projects)' or 'Expert judgment (reason)' or 'Assumption (reason)'. Example: 'Performance risk: High probability (historical: 40% of similar projects experienced performance issues; expert judgment: 1M user target is aggressive for 6-month timeline)'. All probability ratings are justified; no ratings assigned without documented basis.
phase_coverage: Phase 3: Assess Probability and Impact
Phase 4: Calculate Priority and Rank Risks
Phase 7: Compile and Validate Risk Register

test_cases.name: quality_gate_mitigation_action_specificity
test_cases.scenario: Mitigation strategies include specific, actionable steps with named owners and timelines (not vague actions like 'improve documentation').

input.scope_document:
Project: API Platform Redesign
Objectives: Modernize API infrastructure, improve performance, enable real-time data
Deliverables: New API gateway, event streaming system, documentation, migration guide
Constraints: Timeline 5 months, Budget $250K, 4-person team, must maintain backward compatibility
Stakeholders: Platform team, 10 API consumers, DevOps
Success Criteria: 99.99% uptime, <100ms latency, zero breaking changes

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill generates mitigation actions that are specific and imperative. Example: 'Hire API specialist by Week 2' (not 'improve documentation'). 'Conduct load testing with 10M requests/day by Week 8' (not 'ensure performance'). Each action includes: named owner ('John Smith, Tech Lead' not 'team'), timeline ('Start Week 1, complete by Week 4'), cost/effort ('2 weeks, $15K'), success criteria ('Load test passes with <100ms latency at 10M req/day').
phase_coverage: Phase 5: Develop Mitigation Strategies
Phase 7: Compile and Validate Risk Register

test_cases.name: quality_gate_early_warning_indicator_measurability
test_cases.scenario: Early warning indicators are measurable, timely, and actionable with specific thresholds and response triggers.

input.scope_document:
Project: Data Warehouse Migration
Objectives: Migrate from legacy data warehouse to cloud-native platform
Deliverables: Cloud architecture, ETL pipelines, data validation, documentation
Constraints: Timeline 8 months, Budget $600K, 7-person team, zero downtime required
Stakeholders: Data team, Analytics, Finance, IT Operations
Success Criteria: All data migrated, query performance >2x faster, zero data loss

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill generates EWIs that are specific and measurable. Example: 'Schedule risk EWI: Task completion rate drops below 80% of planned (threshold: if <80% for 2 consecutive weeks). Monitoring: weekly, via project management tool. Response: escalate to PM, adjust schedule, activate mitigation.' Not vague like 'monitor closely' or 'team morale is low'. Each EWI includes: measurable indicator (e.g., 'defect density >5 per 1000 LOC'), threshold condition, monitoring frequency (daily/weekly/per-milestone), monitoring method (automated metric, checklist, review), response trigger (specific action).
phase_coverage: Phase 6: Define Early Warning Indicators
Phase 7: Compile and Validate Risk Register

test_cases.name: quality_gate_risk_consistency_validation
test_cases.scenario: Similar risks (same category, similar trigger) have consistent probability and impact ratings; differences are documented.

input.scope_document:
Project: Multi-Phase Product Development
Phase 1: Requirements and design (2 months)
Phase 2: Development (4 months)
Phase 3: Testing and launch (2 months)
Objectives: Launch new product line, capture 15% market share
Deliverables: Product, marketing materials, sales training, support documentation
Constraints: Timeline 8 months, Budget $1M, 15-person team, must launch by Q4 2025
Stakeholders: Product, Engineering, Marketing, Sales, Executive team
Success Criteria: Zero critical bugs, 95% uptime, user adoption >50%

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill identifies similar risks and verifies consistent ratings. Example: 'Resource risk: Key developer unavailable (High probability, High impact). Resource risk: QA lead unavailable (High probability, High impact). Ratings are consistent.' If ratings differ: 'Schedule risk: Phase 1 delay (Medium probability, Medium impact). Schedule risk: Phase 3 delay (Medium probability, High impact - launch impact). Difference documented: Phase 3 delay has higher impact due to market window.' Consistency check is explicit; differences are justified.
phase_coverage: Phase 3: Assess Probability and Impact
Phase 4: Calculate Priority and Rank Risks
Phase 7: Compile and Validate Risk Register

test_cases.name: quality_gate_stakeholder_validation_documented
test_cases.scenario: Risk register is validated by project team and stakeholders; validation is documented with who reviewed, date, and feedback.

input.scope_document:
Project: Customer Portal Redesign
Objectives: Improve user experience, reduce support tickets by 30%, enable self-service
Deliverables: New portal UI, backend services, knowledge base, training
Constraints: Timeline 4 months, Budget $200K, 5-person team, must maintain 99.5% uptime
Stakeholders: Product Manager, Customer Success, Engineering, Support team (8 people)
Success Criteria: NPS >50, support tickets -30%, user adoption >70%
input.stakeholder_input: Support team concerned about knowledge base completeness. Customer Success worried about adoption timeline.

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill documents validation: 'Register validated by: [List who reviewed - names/roles], Date: [ISO date], Feedback: [Summary of feedback]. Concerns raised: [List specific concerns]. Critical risks confirmed: [List risks stakeholders confirmed as important]. Mitigation strategies approved as feasible: [Yes/No, with notes]. Register status: [Approved / Approved with gaps / Requires revision].' Validation is explicit and traceable.
phase_coverage: Phase 7: Compile and Validate Risk Register

phases.phase: Phase 1: Extract and Analyze Project Scope
phases.notes: All test cases exercise scope extraction. tc_003 and tc_005 test error handling for incomplete scope.

phases.phase: Phase 2: Identify Risk Categories and Brainstorm Risks
phases.notes: tc_004 specifically tests consolidation of >100 risks. All others test standard brainstorming.
tc_006 tests probability basis documentation. tc_009 tests consistency validation. tc_002 tests numeric scale (1-5).

phases.phase: Phase 4: Calculate Priority and Rank Risks
phases.notes: All test cases exercise priority calculation. tc_002 tests conservative risk appetite threshold.
tc_007 specifically tests mitigation action specificity and owner naming.
tc_008 specifically tests EWI measurability, thresholds, and response triggers.

phases.phase: Phase 7: Compile and Validate Risk Register
phases.notes: All test cases exercise register compilation. tc_010 specifically tests stakeholder validation documentation.

quality_gates.gate: Phase 1: Scope summary includes all seven elements
quality_gates.notes: Happy path cases verify all elements present. tc_003 and tc_005 test missing elements.

quality_gates.gate: Phase 2: Risks span all applicable categories (minimum 2 per category)
quality_gates.notes: All test cases verify category coverage.

quality_gates.gate: Phase 3: Every probability rating has documented basis
quality_gates.notes: tc_006 specifically tests probability basis documentation.

quality_gates.gate: Phase 3: Similar risks have consistent ratings
Phase 5: Every mitigation strategy has specific action, named owner, timeline, cost, success criteria
quality_gates.notes: tc_007 specifically tests mitigation action specificity.

quality_gates.gate: Phase 6: Every high-priority risk has EWI with measurable threshold and response trigger
quality_gates.notes: tc_008 specifically tests EWI measurability and actionability.

quality_gates.gate: Phase 7: Register is validated by project team (documented: who, date, feedback)

error_handlers.error: Phase 1: Scope document is incomplete or ambiguous
error_handlers.notes: Both test cases verify graceful handling of missing scope elements.
tc_004 specifically tests consolidation and deprioritization.
tc_009 tests consistency validation and documentation of differences.

error_handlers.error: Phase 7: Stakeholder validation reveals missing critical risks
error_handlers.notes: tc_010 tests stakeholder feedback documentation and validation process.
